THE STRANGLER FIG frame (Fowler; Azure 4-phase facade->incremental-shift->decommission->remove-facade; Kinney's frontend specialization): the new system germinates beside the old, behavior moves one vertical slice at a time, both COEXIST, the old is decommissioned once nothing depends on it; the facade is the neutral cae-* surface (Book 12 §2.2 inversion applied to the crossover); the convergence pitfall — if new features keep landing on PrimeNG the migration never converges, so all NEW work goes on Caelum, only bug-fixes on PrimeNG, and the compat layer is scheduled for removal from the start

THE LIVING MAP is the backbone: reference/COMPARISON.md (canonical home; seeded from brief §3) maps p-* -> cae-* -> Material/CDK primitive -> effort tier (Direct/Compose/Build-S/M/L/Adapter[brief's Vet-lib]); rollup = the great majority Direct/Compose, cheap Build-S long tail, effort concentrated in three Adapter rows (grid/charts/editor, Books 13-15) + theming parity; a MAPPED row is NOT a migrated component (Book 16 §2.1 'looks the same is not the same') — a row is a promise to build AND verify at parity, done only at adversarial-passed on the ledger (Book 16 §2.2)

SOURCE-VERSION nuance: PrimeNG renamed components across the 17->21 span a migrating team occupies (Dropdown->Select, Calendar->DatePicker, Sidebar->Drawer, OverlayPanel->Popover, InputSwitch->ToggleSwitch), so the codemod's INPUT side is version-dependent; ~95 current PrimeNG components (real showcase inventory)

MIGRATION CONVERGES via a FORWARD-ONLY FITNESS FUNCTION (Ford/Parsons/Kua 'unit test for architecture'): ESLint no-restricted-imports forbids NEW primeng/* imports (Book 12 §3.3's 'rule that defends itself' generalized from one adapter to a whole library) wrapped in a RATCHET (Betterer/imbue-ai-ratchets/custom count, monotonically decreasing) whose falling count IS the migration burndown to zero; HONEST LIMIT — no-restricted-imports catches TS imports but NOT <p-*> template selectors, so a second leg (an @angular-eslint template rule / codemod-time scan) is required

CODEMOD tooling (stable-since-Angular-6 Schematics architecture; exact v22 CLI surface verify-at-M0): collection.json -> factory (options)=>Rule, Rule=(tree:Tree,ctx)=>Tree, Tree=virtual FS of Create/Rename/Overwrite/Delete actions; TWO rewrite dimensions — TS imports via the TypeScript COMPILER API (what Material/CDK's internal update-tool uses; NOT ts-morph, an added dep Book 03 would vet; update-tool is explicitly NOT public API so imitate-don't-import) + template <p-*> selectors via template-AST/guarded-regex; refactor-jasmine-vitest is v22 first-party prior art; a codemod does the MECHANICAL renames and leaves a TODO(caelum-migrate) MARKER at every semantic prop/event remap a human must decide (§6)

COEXISTENCE hazards during the overlap + mitigations: theming/global-style collisions (drive BOTH Caelum/Material AND residual PrimeNG from the one token bridge, Book 04 — theme-first-components-second), the CDK overlay-container z-index conflict (angular/components#15467; version-sensitive, verify-at-M0 vs the native Popover API shift), and bundle bloat while both ship (MEASURE it with Book 18's two size gates §3.1/§3.2; secondary-entry-point tree-shaking §3.3 means the app carries only adopted cae-*)

SEQUENCING by REAL usage (R7 is a ROADMAP scope note, NOT a brief label — correcting the premise): the brief says grep-your-app-first (§5 Phase 3 / §5 sequencing rule / §9.1 / Appendix A), but Caelum is app-agnostic (D-06) so it has NO app to grep — R7 reframes that as SHIP THE USAGE-MAPPING GUIDE (this book + COMPARISON.md) so the ADOPTING team inventories THEIR p-* usage and re-ranks; first-slice = thin vertical slice, visible value + clear owner + low blast radius (Kinney) balanced against heavily-used-yet-less-complex (Thoughtworks), baseline tests before cutover (dovetails with definition_of_done)

the ADOPTION CHECKLIST defines 'migrated' with evidence not vibes: per-component mapped->renamed(markers resolved)->parity-verified(3 dimensions Book 16 §3.5)->adversarial-passed(ledger, Book 16 §2.2/§3.6)->its primeng count hits zero; per-app the ratchet reaches zero + primeng removed from package.json (leaves the provenance surface, Book 03 §2.3) + the compat layer deleted on schedule (Kinney/Azure phase 4), adapters staying fenced (Book 12 §3.3)

AI leverage split: HIGH on the mechanical bulk (author the codemod, run the usage inventory, repetitive per-component ports, maintain the fitness function) but ~1x on the parity JUDGMENT (does cae-* match the p-* behavior the app USED — the implementer/adversary split Book 16 §2.3, brief §6; the same agent that ran the codemod must not sign off its parity), the first-slice choice, and the semantic prop-remaps the codemod flags; never mark 'migrated' on render alone; no new frontier research note (Schematics API stable-architecture, methodology canonical — Fowler/Azure/Ford/ESLint cited in §8; v22 specifics verify-at-M0)
books.summary: The TWENTIETH and FINAL book — Volume V Book 2, which CLOSES the Distribution & Adoption volume AND completes the 20-book Caelum library. Book 19 made a finished library PUBLISHABLE; this book is the last mile publishing exists to serve: how a team actually LEAVES PrimeNG for Caelum, component by component, without a rewrite. It realizes D-06 (Caelum is an app-agnostic library a team ADOPTS — its authors never see the consuming app) and generalizes D-03/Book 12's adapter isolation from one foreign library to a whole one being removed. THE BOUNDARY with its sibling, stated in both directions: Book 19 §3.4's ng-update migrations upgrade a team ALREADY ON Caelum across Caelum MAJORS (same-package, version-gated); the p-*->cae-* codemods here move a team FROM PrimeNG onto Caelum (cross-package) — so the from-PrimeNG transform is a STANDALONE `ng generate caelum:migrate-from-primeng` Schematic, never an ng-update migration (the load-bearing architectural conclusion, grounded in the real Schematics-surface definitions). The book's spine is a single reframing: a UI-library migration is a STRANGLER FIG (Fowler; Azure's 4-phase facade->incremental-shift->decommission->remove-facade; Steve Kinney's frontend specialization) — the new system germinates beside the old, behavior moves one vertical slice at a time, both COEXIST, and the old host is decommissioned once nothing depends on it; the facade is the neutral cae-* surface (Book 12 §2.2's inversion applied to the crossover), and the thing that makes it CONVERGE rather than stall as 'two libraries forever' is a forward-only fitness function. Four moves. (1) THE LIVING MAP — reference/COMPARISON.md (canonical home, seeded from brief §3) maps every p-* -> cae-* -> the Material/CDK primitive under it -> an effort tier (Direct/Compose/Build-S/M/L/Adapter, the brief's Vet-lib); the rollup is the strategic case (the great majority Direct/Compose, the Build-S long tail individually cheap, effort concentrated in exactly three Adapter rows — grid/charts/editor, Books 13-15 — plus theming parity); but a MAPPED row is NOT a migrated component (Book 16 §2.1 'looks the same is not is the same') — it is a promise to build AND verify across functional/accessible/visual (Book 16 §3.5), done only at adversarial-passed on the capability ledger (Book 16 §2.2). Two honesties: the SOURCE selector is version-dependent (PrimeNG renamed Dropdown->Select, Calendar->DatePicker, Sidebar->Drawer, OverlayPanel->Popover, InputSwitch->ToggleSwitch across 17->21, so the codemod input side must be parameterized), and the map is a starting allocation to correct against actuals with a niche tail (Terminal/OrgChart/Dock/MegaMenu) built-on-demand-or-out-of-scope. (2) COEXISTENCE — two libraries in one app during the overlap, with three designed-against hazards: theming/global-style collisions (drive BOTH Caelum/Material AND residual PrimeNG from the one token bridge, Book 04 — theme first, components second), the CDK overlay-container z-index conflict (angular/components#15467; version-sensitive, verify-at-M0 against the live v22 CDK / the native Popover API shift), and bundle bloat while both ship (MEASURE it with Book 18's two size gates §3.1/§3.2 rather than estimate; secondary-entry-point tree-shaking §3.3 means the app carries only the cae-* it has adopted). (3) THE CODEMODS — mechanical renames on the stable-since-Angular-6 Schematics API (collection.json -> factory (options)=>Rule; Rule=(tree:Tree,ctx)=>Tree; Tree = a virtual FS of Create/Rename/Overwrite/Delete actions; a library advertises its collection via package.json 'schematics'), with TWO rewrite dimensions: TS imports (primeng/* -> caelum/*) via the TypeScript COMPILER API — what Material/CDK's in-repo update-tool uses (creates the ts.Program once, visits each AST once), which is EXPLICITLY NOT public API so Caelum imitates the pattern, and NOT ts-morph (an added third-party dep Book 03 would make you vet, Book 12 §6) — and template <p-*> selectors via template-AST/guarded-regex; refactor-jasmine-vitest is v22 first-party prior art (verify-at-M0). A codemod automates the MECHANICAL renames but CANNOT decide the SEMANTIC remaps (which prop maps to which cae-* input, event-signature diffs, behavior divergence), so it leaves a typed TODO(caelum-migrate) MARKER at every such site, feeding the parity work. (4) CONVERGENCE — an architectural fitness function (Ford/Parsons/Kua, 'an objective integrity assessment of an architectural characteristic', the 'unit test for architecture'): ESLint no-restricted-imports forbids NEW primeng/* imports (Book 12 §3.3's 'rule that defends itself' generalized from one adapter to a whole library), wrapped in a RATCHET (Betterer snapshot / imbue-ai-ratchets budget / custom count, monotonically decreasing) so backsliding is a build failure and the falling count of remaining forbidden imports IS the migration burndown to zero; the HONEST LIMIT — no-restricted-imports catches TS imports but NOT <p-*> template selectors, so a second leg (an @angular-eslint template rule or codemod-time scan) is required or the migration silently backslides in templates. SEQUENCING by REAL usage: R7 is a ROADMAP SCOPE NOTE, not a brief label (the brief has no R7 and assumes the team greps its own app — §5/§9.1/Appendix A); Caelum being app-agnostic has NO app to grep (D-06), so R7 reframes grep-your-app-first as SHIP THE USAGE-MAPPING GUIDE (this book + COMPARISON.md) — the adopting team inventories THEIR p-* usage and re-ranks; first-slice = a thin vertical slice with visible value + a clear owner + low blast radius (Kinney) balanced against heavily-used-yet-less-complex (Thoughtworks), with baseline functional/a11y/perf tests before cutover (dovetails with definition_of_done). The ADOPTION CHECKLIST defines 'migrated' with evidence: per-component mapped -> renamed (every marker resolved) -> parity-verified (three dimensions) -> adversarial-passed (the ledger, the same separate-party-mandated-to-falsify as brief §6) -> its primeng count hits zero; per-app the ratchet reaches zero + primeng removed from package.json (so it leaves the provenance surface entirely, Book 03 §2.3, the whole point) + the coexistence/compat layer deleted on the schedule set at the start (Kinney 'remove the facade' / Azure phase 4), with the admitted adapters staying fenced (Book 12 §3.3). §4 sketches the artifacts (a COMPARISON.md row, the caelum collection.json with ng-add + migrate-from-primeng, the migrate rule with the TODO markers, the forward-only ESLint config, the template-leg note) with a verify-at-M0 banner. AI integration draws the leverage line: HIGH on the mechanical bulk (authoring the codemod, the usage inventory, the repetitive ports, maintaining the fitness function) and ~1x on the parity JUDGMENT (does cae-select match the p-select behavior the app USED — Book 16 §3.6 / brief §6; the same agent that ran the codemod must NOT sign off its parity, the implementer/adversary split Book 16 §2.3), the first-slice choice, and the semantic prop-remaps the codemod flags; never mark a component 'migrated' on render alone. No new frontier research note (the Schematics API is stable-architecture and the methodology is canonical — Fowler, Azure, Ford/Parsons/Kua, ESLint, angular.dev cited in §8; version-specific Angular/CDK/PrimeNG specifics — the exact v22 CLI/schematics surface, the @angular-eslint template-rule name, the overlay/coexistence mechanics, selectorless components — are verify-at-M0). CLOSES Volume V and the curriculum — the twentieth and FINAL book.

Library-grounded implementation plan; the planning front-end.
Refine a milestone/epic into grounded, dependency-ordered slice tickets at milestone start (the horizon rule).
Conformance audit of a design/diff vs the library, cited Must-fix/Consider.
Multi-lens independent review before merge; fans out reviewers to falsify claims.
Run the full verification gate with per-gate evidence.
Full build + test cycle using PROJECT_CONVENTIONS commands.
Add unit/integration/property/golden tests.
Capture and analyze a profile for a named subsystem.
Profile-driven optimization of a hot path.
Agentic LLM content-generation pipeline with validation.
Add a telemetry event with schema + sampling.
Add a typed, validated, permission-filtered MCP tool.
Round-trip snapshot/restore test for any state-owning subsystem.
CI gate: initializes/ticks/shuts down with no window/device/human.
Ensure deferred work lives in the tracker, not only in chat.
Convert free-form `inbox` tickets (human requests, downstream sync reports) into process-rigorous tickets with a disposition receipt; untrusted-input guards; never starts build work.
Survey a frontier topic online into a sourced + tiered research note (real fetched links only).
Pre-registered, reproducible experiment with comparable, plottable results (EXP-NN).
Markdown-first paper artifact (RR-NN) with real-link references and regenerable figures.
CMMI-L5 defect-prevention pass: mine escaped defects + metrics, root-cause, leave a guard (gate/anti-pattern/lens) not just a fix.
Whole-repo over-engineering sweep (delete/stdlib/native/yagni/shrink), biggest-cut-first ledger + net tally; files debt tickets, never deletes. Complements adversarial_review's per-diff lens.

coverage_gaps.description: Topics the library does NOT cover or covers only partially. Check here FIRST when asked about something hard to locate, and say so honestly rather than fabricate. As of 2026-06-30 Volume I (Books 01-04: Angular 22 architecture; reactivity & forms; provenance & licensing; the theming token bridge), Volume II (Books 05-11: CDK Primitives; Angular Aria Headless Primitives; Form Control Fundamentals; Numeric/Mask/Specialized Inputs; Overlay & Menu Components; Data Tables & Virtualization; Layout/Panels/Media & Drag-Drop), and Volume III (Books 12-15: The Adapter Pattern; Data Grid Adapter; Charts Adapter; Rich-Text Editor Adapter) are ALL COMPLETE — the full component-level curriculum AND the whole adapter layer are authored. Volume III: Book 12 covers the adapter PATTERN (neutral interface, the only-the-adapter-touches-it rule, ESLint isolation, DI swappability), Book 13 the first CONCRETE adapter (the headless TanStack grid), Book 14 the second (render-it-yourself D3 SVG charts — visx is React-bound, refining D-08 to D3-direct), and Book 15 the third, COMPLETING the volume (the Lexical rich-text editor — whose core, the favorable mirror of visx, is framework-agnostic with no React peer, so D-09 stands as-is; the editor is also a form control via CVA). Volume IV (Quality) is now COMPLETE (3/3): Book 16 (Accessibility & Parity Verification — the capability ledger, pre-committed parity scenarios, the implementer-vs-adversarial split, and the three verification layers axe + keyboard + screen reader, with the lesson that axe-core is MPL-2.0, admissible only as a dev/test tool), Book 17 (Testing Strategy & Tooling — the test pyramid bent for a component library, the FRONTIER fact that Angular 22's default unit-test runner is now Vitest with Karma legacy, the CDK ComponentHarness depth, unit/component/integration/visual layers, Playwright + the golden discipline, the whole toolchain as dev-tier provenance), and Book 18 (Performance & Bundle Budgets — performance as a budgeted/gated dimension profiled-not-guessed; the TWO size gates, app angular.json budgets vs the shipped-library per-entry-point gate; tree-shaking via APF/sideEffects/secondary-entry-points; @defer lazy loading; zoneless/OnPush CD proven with the profiler) are authored. Volume V (Distribution & Adoption) is now COMPLETE (2/2): Book 19 (Packaging, Versioning & Distribution — @angular/build:ng-packagr + the Angular Package Format, the exports map + secondary entry points, @angular/* peer deps + lockstep semver, ng-update migrations, and npm/SLSA build provenance vs the separately-shipped US-origin attestation — the two orthogonal provenances) and Book 20 (Migration & Adoption — the living p-*->cae-* component map in reference/COMPARISON.md, the strangler-fig incremental adoption, the standalone `ng generate caelum:migrate-from-primeng` codemods distinct from Book 19's ng-update, and the forward-only lint-ratchet fitness function that converges the migration and prevents adapter erosion) are authored. THE 20-BOOK CAELUM LIBRARY IS COMPLETE — all five volumes, Books 01-20. Update each entry as its book lands.
